name: GLM-5.1
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts, 744B total / 40B active, DeepSeek Sparse Attention (DSA), 200K context, tuned for autonomous long-horizon agent runs (up to 8 hours single-run)
note: Point release focused on extreme long-horizon agentic engineering; Z.ai's own SWE-bench Pro claim (58.4) briefly topped the public leaderboard, edging out GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus 4.6 on that benchmark at launch.

results.notes: SWE-bench Pro; Z.ai claimed this briefly topped the global leaderboard ahead of GPT-5.4 (57.7) and Claude Opus 4.6 (57.3) at launch

Humanity's Last Exam, text-only default subset (no tools). Vendor footnote: temperature=1.0, top_p=0.95, max_new_tokens=163840, GPT-5.2 (medium) as judge. Identical value appears on the HF model card (huggingface.co/zai-org/GLM-5.1). Distinct from the model's 'HLE w/ Tools' score of 52.3, which is a different evaluation config with no matching taxonomy slug and was not mapped here to avoid variant conflation.

GDPval-AA v2, Artificial Analysis's independent Elo-based real-world-work-task eval (normalized as (Elo-500)/2000), one of 9 components in their Intelligence Index v4.1. Read directly from AA's live ranked bar chart (GLM-5.1 ranked 12th of 28 models shown, tied at 38% with MiMo-V2.5-Pro and Nex-N2-Pro). Not self-reported by Z.ai. AA continuously re-runs/updates this eval, so the score may shift on future snapshots; some secondary press citing an earlier v1/Elo-scale run (~1535 Elo) is not directly comparable to this v2 percentage figure.

IFBench (instruction-following), one of 9 components in Artificial Analysis's Intelligence Index v4.1. Read directly from AA's live ranked bar chart (GLM-5.1 tied 8th of 23 models at 76%). Not self-reported by Z.ai; GLM-5.1 was never evaluated on IFBench in its own launch materials.

verificationNotes: Confirmed real via Hugging Face model card (zai-org/GLM-5.1, MIT license), official docs.z.ai/guides/llm/glm-5.1 (200K context, 128K max output, SWE-bench Pro 58.4 claim), zai-org/GLM-5 GitHub repo (which lists GLM-5.1 alongside GLM-5/5.2 at 744B/40B), and independent coverage (BuildFastWithAI, DeepInfra blog, DeepLearning.AI's The Batch, Wikipedia GLM(AI) article) all dating the release to April 7, 2026 and describing its signature 'runs autonomously for up to 8 hours, re-evaluating its own plan hundreds of times' capability. The Hugging Face page's auto-surfaced 'release date' metadata (derived from the shared arXiv paper 2602.15763) incorrectly implied a February date -- overridden in favor of the April 7, 2026 date that is explicitly and consistently reported by multiple independent outlets describing the actual product launch. Params: HF card says '754 billion total' while GitHub repo says 744B -- used 744B (matches GLM-5 and GLM-5.2 in the same repo table) and flagged the 10B discrepancy as likely rounding/estimation noise between sources. Benchmark scores (SWE-bench Pro, Terminal-Bench 2.0, CyberGym, BrowseComp, AIME 2026, GPQA-Diamond) taken directly from the Hugging Face model card's self-reported table. Omitted NL2Repo (42.7), HMMT Nov 2025 (94.0), HMMT Feb 2026 (82.6), IMOAnswerBench (83.8), Tool-Decathlon (40.7), and MCP-Atlas (71.8) for lack of a matching corpus benchmark slug.
